ELPAC Level 2

DTR | Listen to a Speaker Support an Opinion
LSO-MS6-L2-1400P-MSMATH6-MCF-GBL-02 | MS Math: Grade 6 | Statistical Variability (Mean, Median, IQR, MAD) | Mystery Case File | Global Perspectives
Cracking Thirteen
Click 🎧 to listen.
Listen to a conversation between two students. The boy is discussing his opinion about how to share the data from their global partner schools. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer four questions about the conversation and the evidence the boy gives to support his opinion. As you listen, you may take notes.
Listen to the Conversation
1 QUESTION 1:
Why does the boy bring up the poster's headline?
2 QUESTION 2:
Where did the boy get the numbers he used to question the headline?
3 QUESTION 3:
According to the boy, why is the average higher than the middle number?
4 QUESTION 4:
What is the boy's main reason for wanting to use the middle number and show the spread?
Answer all 4 questions to submit.
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. What is the boy trying to accomplish across the whole conversation, and how does his choice to compare the average with the middle number shape what he concludes about the cultures?
CA ELD: PI.A.3, PI.B.5, PI.B.7, PI.B.8, PII.A.1 | CCSS: 6.SP.A.3, 6.SP.B.5.c | MS Math: Grade 6
